title: Early life exposure to house dust mite allergen prevents experimental allergic asthma requiring mitochondrial H(2)O(2).
ehr_evidence: The study is an experimental animal model using mice, not human electronic health records.
summary: An experimental study investigating the role of mitochondrial H2O2 in establishing airway tolerance to house dust mite (HDM) allergens in mice.
key_findings: Early-life exposure to low-dose HDM prevents allergic airway inflammation in later life.
Depletion of mitochondrial H2O2 in MCAT mice abrogates HDM-induced airway tolerance.
Mitochondrial H2O2 plays a role in constraining lung dendritic cell numbers to establish early-life airway tolerance.
